Physics Worker Test

Results will appear here...

LLM Agent Usage Guide

This page demonstrates how to use the Standalone Physics Worker for the Billiards engine. It is designed to be easily incorporated into external web applications or tools managed by AI agents.

1. Include the Worker

You can instantiate the worker from the following public URL:

const worker = new Worker('https://tailuge.github.io/billiards/dist/worker.js');

2. Input Specification (SimulationConfig)

Send a message to the worker with the following structure:

{
  "ruleType": "threecushion", // "threecushion" or "nineball" (sets geometry/pockets)
  "balls": [
    { "id": 0, "pos": { "x": -0.5, "y": 0, "z": 0 } } // Required: id and pos {x, y, z}
  ],
  "cushionModel": "mathavan",   // "mathavan" or "han"
  "shot": {
    "cueBallId": 0,
    "angle": 0,                // Radians
    "power": 0.5,              // Magnitude
    "offset": { "x": 0, "y": 0 }, // Spin offset (-0.3 to 0.3)
    "elevation": 0             // Cue tilt in radians
  },
  "stepSize": 0.01,            // Physics timestep
  "maxIterations": 10          // Safety limit for the loop
}

3. Output Specification (SIM_COMPLETE)

The worker responds with a raw JSON object:

{
  "type": "SIM_COMPLETE",
  "computeTime": "15ms",
  "frames": [
    {
      "t": 10,
      "balls": [{ "id": 0, "pos": [x,y,z], "rvel": [wx,wy,wz], "state": "Sliding" }]
    }
  ],
  "outcomes": [
    { "type": "Collision", "ballA": 0, "ballB": 1, "speed": 5.2, "t": 250 }
  ]
}

4. Attribution

If you use this physics utility in your project, kindly include a link back to the original repository:
https://github.com/tailuge/billiards
